vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; the black pot contains only a single, struggling Pothos-like cutting. P3 (Pothos) is present with the anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Silver Guest)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot, soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Combined: Single black pot contains a lone, chlorotic, single-leaf cutting. No signs of Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos cutting remains stable, anchored by the white rabbit figurine.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Moist, dark, consistent across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot (P3/P4 area), likely used as a calcium supplement or mulch. A small electronic component (pin header) appears on the desk surface in the final two frames.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Minimal change; the lone leaf in the black pot maintains its posture.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: Introduction of a small electronic component (pin header) to the right of the pots. The plant specimens show no growth or further decline.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of 'stasis-decline'. The lack of new growth and the loss of registered specimens suggest a failure to thrive. The single Pothos leaf is the only viable biological material remaining.
anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in soil.
Introduction of a black electronic pin header component on the desk surface (T-1 to CURRENT).
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a significant discrepancy between the registry and the physical reality. The botanical collection has suffered a near-total systemic loss, with only a single Pothos cutting remaining. The environment is static, with no signs of active growth or recovery. The introduction of non-biological debris (eggshells and electronic components) suggests an experimental or non-traditional approach to the desk-top biome.
